questions: Identify the digital resource that serves as the major output of the joint IUSSP and UNFPA project dedicated to providing updated tools for demographic estimation from limited and defective data, framed as a direct successor to the UN Manual X. Within this resource, locate the section authored by T.A. Moultrie specifically concerning the 'Evaluation and correction of fertility data.' Provide the full suggested citation for this section, including the publication year, the full list of editors for the parent work, and the publisher's location.

rounds.answer_a:
The major digital resource is the online tool **Demographic Estimation Tools (DET)**, formally titled *Tools for Demographic Estimation*. This platform, developed by the IUSSP and UNFPA, is the direct successor to the UN Manual X. The section authored by T.A. Moultrie on the "Evaluation and correction of fertility data" is found within Part II, Chapter 2, of this resource [1]. The suggested full citation for the specific chapter is: Moultrie, T.A. 2013. "Evaluation and correction of fertility data." In *Tools for Demographic Estimation*, edited by T. Moultrie, R. Dorrington, A. Hill, K. Hill, I. Timæus, and B. Zaba. Paris: International Union for the Scientific Study of Population (IUSSP) [1].

## References
[1] Moultrie, T.A. 2013. "Evaluation and correction of fertility data." In *Tools for Demographic Estimation*, edited by T. Moultrie, R. Dorrington, A. Hill, K. Hill, I. Timæus, and B. Zaba. Paris: International Union for the Scientific Study of Population (IUSSP). https://demographicestimation.iussp.org/content/evaluation-and-correction-fertility-data
rounds.answer_b:
The digital resource is **Tools for Demographic Estimation**, a web-based platform hosted by the IUSSP that serves as the major output of the joint IUSSP and UNFPA project[1][5]. It directly succeeds UN Manual X and provides methods for estimating demographic parameters from limited and defective data[5].

The resource contains a section on "Evaluation and assessment of data" within its structured navigation[8], though the search results do not provide the complete citation details for T.A. Moultrie's specific section on "Evaluation and correction of fertility data." The parent work was published in 2013 (second impression) and edited by Moultrie, Dorrington, Hill, Hill, Timæus, and Zaba[3]. The resource is freely accessible online at demographicestimation.iussp.org following registration[5].

For the precise citation including publisher location and Moultrie's specific fertility data section, direct consultation of the Tools for Demographic Estimation website or print version is necessary[5].

## References

[1] https://iussp.org/en/tools-demographic-estimation-0

[3] https://demographicestimation.iussp.org/sites/default/files/2023-05/TDE_2013_2ndImpression_opt_0.pdf

[5] https://demographicestimation.iussp.org/content/introduction-tools-demographic-estimation

[8] https://demographicestimation.iussp.org/content/tools-demographic-estimation
